prerequisites: A running Airbyte deployment on the local workstation where the destination files should be written. The Local JSON destination is meant for local use and will not work on Kubernetes.
On Docker Desktop (for example macOS), file sharing must grant the Docker engine access to the host directories used for output (such as /tmp and /private) so the connector can write files.
A value for the required destination_path configuration field, which always starts with /local.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The Local JSON destination requires no credentials or secrets. It writes JSON files directly to the local filesystem where Airbyte runs, so the connector descriptor defines no secret fields. The only required setting is the destination_path config field.

setupSteps.title: Enable Docker file sharing for the output location
setupSteps.body: Because the connector writes to the host filesystem through Docker, ensure the Docker engine can access the target directories. On Docker Desktop, grant file-sharing access (for example to /tmp and /private on macOS) under Settings then Resources then File sharing. By default output is written under /tmp/airbyte_local, controlled by the LOCAL_ROOT environment variable in Airbyte's .env file.

setupSteps.title: Set the destination_path
setupSteps.body: Provide the required destination_path field. The path always starts with /local, and the /local prefix is mapped to Airbyte's LOCAL_ROOT directory on the host. For example, destination_path of /local/cars/models writes files to /tmp/airbyte_local/cars/models when LOCAL_ROOT is the default /tmp/airbyte_local.

setupSteps.title: Create the destination and sync
setupSteps.body: Add the Local JSON destination in Airbyte using the configured destination_path, then connect it to a source and run a sync. Each stream is written to its own file containing JSON objects with the fields _airbyte_ab_id, _airbyte_emitted_at, and _airbyte_data.
